sentence: Subjective:

The patient, a 65-year-old female with a history of coronary artery disease, hypertension, and diabetes, presents for follow-up after an interior ST-elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI) where another stent was placed.
She reports experiencing similar symptoms to her previous heart attack, including chest pain that did not resolve.
The patient denies chest pain and shortness of breath.
She reports being able to exercise by walking her dog daily, approximately 1.5 to 2 miles.
She has a history of leg swelling since hospital discharge.
The patient affirms taking her prescribed medications and has good insurance coverage.
She admits to not strictly adhering to her salt-restricted diet, citing a fondness for pizza and sausage.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of coronary artery disease, hypertension, and diabetes.
Review of systems:
The patient denies any additional symptoms in other organs.
Current medications:
Aspirin 81mg daily, Brilinta 90mg twice daily, Lipitor 80mg daily, To-prol 50mg daily, Metformin (for diabetes), Lisinopril 20mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not explicitly mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's neck exam is unremarkable for jugular venous distention and carotid bruits.
The heart exam reveals a 3/6 systolic ejection murmur at the left base.
The lungs are clear.
The patient has lower extremity edema with 1+ pitting edema on the right radial artery.
The cath site is clean, dry, and intact without hematoma, and the right radial artery pulses are palpable.
Test Results:
EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, good R wave progression, and evolutionary changes expected after a STEMI.
Echo: Reduced ejection fraction of 35%.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has coronary artery disease, newly reduced left ventricular dysfunction, moderate mitral regurgitation, and acute heart failure.
Problem list: Coronary artery disease (active); Reduced left ventricular dysfunction (suspected); Mitral regurgitation (moderate); Acute heart failure (active).
Plan:
I will continue the patient on Aspirin 81mg daily, Brilinta 90mg twice daily, Lipitor 80mg daily, To-prol 50mg daily, and Lisinopril 20mg daily.
I will refer the patient to cardiac rehab for education and exercise guidance.
I will prescribe Lasix 40mg daily and Aldactone 12.5mg daily for heart failure management.
I will repeat an echo in 2 months.
Follow-up:
I will see the patient again in 2 months to monitor progress and adjust the treatment plan as necessary.
